topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the ballroom felt thick, suffocating, like breathing through damp velvet. I kept my eyes fixed on the crystal chandelier, counting the facets of glass, anything to avoid the periphery. Then, there he was. Standing near the buffet, laughing with an ease that felt like a physical blow. It was Mark. He looked… polished. Too polished. The familiar tightening in my chest started immediately, a cold, hard knot forming beneath my ribs. I forced a sip of my champagne, the bubbles tasting metallic and sharp. When his gaze swept across the room and landed on mine, for a fraction of a second, I felt a profound sense of nausea rise up. I quickly looked away, my jaw clenching so hard my teeth ached. I needed air. I needed to be anywhere but this suffocating display of forced happiness.

[story 2]

I’d spent the last hour strategically positioning myself near the exit, pretending to examine the floral arrangements. Every time the music swelled, I braced myself, anticipating the inevitable collision. And there he was, across the room, draped in a tuxedo that somehow made him look even more arrogant. Seeing him, a ghost from a life I desperately tried to outrun, sent a wave of unpleasant revulsion washing over me. My stomach lurched. I felt an urgent need to physically retreat, to dissolve into the wallpaper. I gripped my glass so tightly my knuckles were white. When our eyes met—a brief, unwelcome acknowledgment—I couldn't stop the subtle curl of my lip, a purely involuntary reaction of repulsion. I focused intensely on the pattern of the carpet, willing myself to become invisible.

[story 3]

The noise of the celebration was a relentless assault, a cacophony of forced cheer and clinking silverware. I was trying to be polite, nodding at acquaintances, but my focus kept snapping back to one specific point in the room: the corner where he stood. He looked entirely too comfortable, radiating an aura of smug contentment that grated on my nerves like sandpaper. A strange heat began to bloom in my neck, spreading outward, making the fine hairs on my arms prickle. I watched him move, and the familiar, unwelcome memory of our past flashed through my mind, bringing with it a heavy, unpleasant weight. I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to regulate my breathing, but the sensation in my gut remained unsettled, a churning, unwelcome turmoil that made me want to flee the entire event immediately.
